missing_info: Provide the exact timestamp(s) of the alerted state changes for: R2 Serial0/1/1 (up -> lower-layer-down) and R3 Serial0/1/1 (up -> down/admin down), and the alert source/system that reported them.

Provide recent interface operational history and logs (syslog, SNMP traps or interface event records) around those timestamps for R2 Serial0/1/1 and R3 Serial0/1/1 (examples: output of 'show interfaces Serial0/1/1', 'show logging | include Serial0/1/1', or the provider circuit alarm logs).

Provide configuration-change/audit records covering the time window (who/when) and the current running-config snippet for interface Serial0/1/1 on R2 and R3 to confirm if 'shutdown' was applied/removed on R3.

Confirm whether any scheduled maintenance or provider/carrier activities were taking place on the serial links at the alert time, and whether the alert was a single occurrence or repeated/flapping events.

plan_history.intent_description: R2 Serial0/1/1 went from oper=up to oper=lower-layer-down while admin remains up; R3 Serial0/1/1 went from oper=up to oper=down and is administratively down. Impact: loss of serial link between R2 and R3. Desired outcome: restore the serial link (oper up) by reconciling administrative state.
plan_history.problem: Serial link between R2 and R3 is down: R2 Serial0/1/1 oper=lower-layer-down (admin up); R3 Serial0/1/1 oper=down (admin down). Likely cause: R3 interface is administratively shut.
plan_history.fix_summary: Remove administrative shutdown on R3 Serial0/1/1 (set admin up) to restore the link. If link does not return to oper up, perform further diagnostics (log review, provider circuit checks).
